content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 10, 1988

Patty Jo Rutland
Deputy Administrative Officer
Assembly Rules Committee
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA    95814

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-88-070

Dear Ms. Rutland:

     This will confirm our telephone conversation of this date
regarding implementation of the amended provisions of
Government Code Section 89001 restricting the use of
newsletters and mass mailings by elected public officials.

     The "nomination documents" referred to in 89001(a), which
applies to incumbent members of the State assembly, means all
documents necessary to have a candidate's name printed on the
ballot.

     The Commission has noticed amendments to 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18902 to codify this interpretation. A
copy of the noticed regulation is enclosed. It will be
considered at the Commission's hearing on April 5, 1988, in
Sacramento.

     If I can be of further assistance in this matter, please
don't hesitate to contact me at 322-5901.

Sincerely,


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division

REL:LS:jaj

Enclosure


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: What does 'nomination documents' mean under Government Code section 89001(a) as it applies to incumbent state assembly members regarding restrictions on use of newsletters and mass mailings?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The 'nomination documents' referred to in Government Code section 89001(a) means all documents necessary to have a candidate's name printed on the ballot. The FPPC has noticed amendments to California Code of Regulations section 18902 to codify this interpretation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers. The question and conclusion were synthesized from the body text. Telephone conversation confirmation letter with regulatory amendment reference.

QUESTION: What does 'nomination documents' mean under Government Code section 89001(a) as it applies to incumbent state assembly members regarding restrictions on use of newsletters and mass mailings?

CONCLUSION: The 'nomination documents' referred to in Government Code section 89001(a) means all documents necessary to have a candidate's name printed on the ballot. The FPPC has noticed amendments to California Code of Regulations section 18902 to codify this interpretation.

The "nomination documents" referred to in 89001(a), which applies to incumbent members of the State assembly, means all documents necessary to have a candidate's name printed on the ballot. The Commission has noticed amendments to 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18902 to codify this interpretation. A copy of the noticed regulation is enclosed. It will be considered at the Commission's hearing on April 5, 1988, in Sacramento. If I can be of further assistance in this matter, please don't hesitate to contact me at 322-5901. embedding.summary: FPPC advises that 'nomination documents' under Government Code section 89001(a) restricting newsletter and mass mailing use by incumbent state assembly members means all documents necessary for ballot access. The FPPC has proposed regulatory amendments to codify this interpretation.
